title: rubia peregrina subsp. tinctorum
intro: Rubia peregrina subsp. tinctorum, also known as the common madder or dyer's madder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubia peregrina subsp. tinctorum include reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, as well as its potential to have anticancer and antidiabetic properties. Rubia peregrina subsp. tinctorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used as a dye, and its root is often used in traditional Chinese medicine.
